methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Impulse Labs is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Sam D'Amico that has raised $25M to date. The valuation universe comprises 9 private peers in the Climate & Energy sector, including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, plus 1 public comparable (LNZA).

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-set comparables at the same or adjacent stage with similar valuation tier; the 9 same-sector peers did not yield the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable triangulation. Consequently, analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $25M total raised proxy, implying a $50M valuation at the next stage.

The triangulated range spans $42M (low) to $57M (high), with a median of $50M, derived from a single applicable method. This range should be treated with caution given the methodological constraints. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific frontier-tech subsector and the absence of direct peer benchmarking to validate stage-progression assumptions. A more robust valuation would require either broader sector comparables or direct revenue/traction data to cross-check the implied multiples.
